   452→    requests = [r for r in records if r.get("type") == "request"]
   453→    analysis.api_calls = len(requests)
   454→    if not requests:
   455→        return analysis
   456→
   457→    # Tool usage
   458→    tool_use_counts = _collect_tool_uses(records)
   459→    tool_def_bytes = _infer_tool_definition_bytes(records)
   460→    analysis.tool_usage = ToolUsageReport(
   461→        defined_tools=list(_KNOWN_TOOLS),
   462→        used_tools=sorted(tool_use_counts.keys()),
   463→        unused_tools=sorted(
   464→            set(_KNOWN_TOOLS) - set(tool_use_counts.keys())
   465→        ),
   466→        tool_use_counts=tool_use_counts,
   467→        tool_definition_bytes=tool_def_bytes,
   468→    )
   469→    analysis.total_tool_definition_bytes = tool_def_bytes * len(requests)
   470→
   471→    # Component tracking across turns
   472→    tracks: dict[str, ComponentTrack] = {}
   473→
   474→    for turn_idx, rec in enumerate(requests):
   475→        sp = rec.get("system_prompt_full", [])
   476→        msgs = rec.get("messages_full", [])
   477→
   478→        # Extract components
   479→        sp_components = _extract_sp_components(
   480→            sp if isinstance(sp, list) else []
   481→        )
   482→        msg_components = _extract_message_injections(msgs)
   483→
   484→        # System prompt bytes
   485→        sp_bytes = sum(c.bytes for c in sp_components)
   486→        analysis.total_system_prompt_bytes += sp_bytes
   487→
   488→        # Message injection bytes
   489→        inj_bytes = sum(c.bytes for c in msg_components)
   490→        analysis.total_message_injection_bytes += inj_bytes
   491→
   492→        analysis.total_request_bytes += rec.get("total_request_bytes", 0)
   493→
   494→        # Track each component
   495→        all_components = sp_components + msg_components
   496→        for comp in all_components:
   497→            if comp.name not in tracks:
   498→                tracks[comp.name] = ComponentTrack(name=comp.name)
   499→            tracks[comp.name].hashes.append(comp.content_hash)
   500→            tracks[comp.name].sizes.append(comp.bytes)
   501→
   502→        # Duplicate detection (skills within this turn)
   503→        if turn_idx == 0:
   504→            dupe_groups = _find_duplicate_skills(msg_components)
   505→            analysis.duplicate_groups = dupe_groups
   506→            analysis.duplicate_bytes = sum(
   507→                g.duplicate_bytes for g in dupe_groups
   508→            )
   509→
   510→        # Sample decomposition (first turn)
   511→        if turn_idx == 0:
   512→            analysis.sample_decomposition = [
   513→                {"name": c.name, "source": c.source, "bytes": c.bytes}
   514→                for c in all_components
   515→            ]
   516→
   517→    # Aggregate static/dynamic
   518→    for track in tracks.values():
   519→        if track.is_static:
   520→            analysis.static_bytes += track.total_bytes_sent
   521→            analysis.static_wasted_bytes += track.wasted_bytes
   522→        else:
   523→            analysis.dynamic_bytes += track.total_bytes_sent
   524→
   525→    analysis.component_tracks = sorted(
   526→        tracks.values(), key=lambda t: t.total_bytes_sent, reverse=True
   527→    )
   528→
   529→    return analysis
   530→
   531→
   532→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   533→# Display
   534→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   535→
   536→def print_analysis(a: SessionAnalysis) -> None:
   537→    """Print human-readable analysis."""
   538→    print(f"\n{'=' * 65}")
   539→    print("SYSTEM PROMPT WASTE ANALYSIS")
   540→    print(f"{'=' * 65}")
   541→    print(f"Log: {a.proxy_log}")
   542→    print(f"API calls: {a.api_calls}")
   543→
   544→    # Component decomposition (first turn)
   545→    print(f"\n--- Component Decomposition (Turn 1) ---")
   546→    for comp in a.sample_decomposition:
   547→        print(f"  {comp['name']:<35s} {comp['bytes']:>8,} bytes  ({comp['source']})")
   548→
   549→    # Static vs dynamic
   550→    total_content = a.static_bytes + a.dynamic_bytes
   551→    print(f"\n--- Static vs Dynamic ---")
   552→    print(f"  Static content:       {a.static_bytes:>12,} bytes  ({_pct(a.static_bytes, total_content)})")
   553→    print(f"  Dynamic content:      {a.dynamic_bytes:>12,} bytes  ({_pct(a.dynamic_bytes, total_content)})")
   554→    print(f"  Static re-send waste: {a.static_wasted_bytes:>12,} bytes")
   555→
   556→    # Per-component tracking
   557→    print(f"\n--- Per-Component Tracking ---")
   558→    print(f"  {'Component':<35s} {'Total':>10s} {'Turns':>6s} {'Changes':>8s} {'Static':>7s}")
   559→    print(f"  {'-'*35} {'-'*10} {'-'*6} {'-'*8} {'-'*7}")
   560→    for t in a.component_tracks:
   561→        static_label = "yes" if t.is_static else "no"
   562→        print(
   563→            f"  {t.name:<35s} {t.total_bytes_sent:>10,} "
   564→            f"{t.turns_present:>6d} {t.change_count:>8d} {static_label:>7s}"
   565→        )
   566→
   567→    # Tool usage
   568→    tu = a.tool_usage
   569→    print(f"\n--- Tool Usage ---")
   570→    print(f"  Tool definition overhead: {tu.tool_definition_bytes:>10,} bytes/request")
   571→    print(f"  Total across session:     {a.total_tool_definition_bytes:>10,} bytes")
   572→    print(f"  Defined tools:  {len(tu.defined_tools)}")
   573→    print(f"  Used tools:     {len(tu.used_tools)}  {tu.used_tools}")
   574→    print(f"  Unused tools:   {len(tu.unused_tools)}  {tu.unused_tools}")
   575→    if tu.defined_tools:
   576→        print(f"  Est. unused tool bytes:   {a.unused_tool_bytes:>10,} bytes/request")
   577→    if tu.tool_use_counts:
   578→        print(f"\n  Tool call counts:")
   579→        for name, count in sorted(
   580→            tu.tool_use_counts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]
   581→        ):
   582→            print(f"    {name:<25s} {count:>6d}")
   583→
   584→    # Duplicates
   585→    if a.duplicate_groups:
   586→        print(f"\n--- Duplicate Content ---")
   587→        print(f"  Total duplicate bytes (per turn): {a.duplicate_bytes:>10,}")
   588→        for g in sorted(a.duplicate_groups, key=lambda x: -x.duplicate_bytes):
   589→            print(
   590→                f"  {g.canonical_name:<30s} "
   591→                f"x{len(g.members)} copies, "
   592→                f"{g.duplicate_bytes:>6,} bytes wasted"
   593→            )
   594→            for m in g.members:
   595→                print(f"    - {m}")
   596→
   597→    # Summary
   598→    print(f"\n--- Session Summary ---")
   599→    print(f"  Total request bytes:          {a.total_request_bytes:>12,}")
   600→    print(f"  System prompt bytes:          {a.total_system_prompt_bytes:>12,}  ({_pct(a.total_system_prompt_bytes, a.total_request_bytes)})")
   601→    print(f"  Message injection bytes:      {a.total_message_injection_bytes:>12,}  ({_pct(a.total_message_injection_bytes, a.total_request_bytes)})")
   602→    print(f"  Tool definition bytes:        {a.total_tool_definition_bytes:>12,}  ({_pct(a.total_tool_definition_bytes, a.total_request_bytes)})")
   603→    print(f"  Total overhead:               {a.total_overhead_bytes:>12,}  ({_pct(a.total_overhead_bytes, a.total_request_bytes)})")
   604→    print(f"  Static re-send waste:         {a.static_wasted_bytes:>12,}")
   605→    print(f"  Duplicate waste (per turn):   {a.duplicate_bytes:>12,}")
   606→    print(f"  Unused tool waste (per req):  {a.unused_tool_bytes:>12,}")
   607→
   608→
   609→def _pct(part: int, whole: int) -> str:
   610→    if whole == 0:
   611→        return "0.0%"
   612→    return f"{part / whole:.1%}"
   613→
   614→
   615→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   616→# CLI
   617→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   618→
   619→def main():
   620→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   621→        description="Analyze system prompt waste in proxy JSONL logs"
   622→    )
   623→    parser.add_argument(
   624→        "proxy_log",
   625→        type=Path,
   626→        help="Path to proxy JSONL log file",
   627→    )
   628→    parser.add_argument(
   629→        "--json", action="store_true",
   630→        help="Output as JSON",
   631→    )
   632→    args = parser.parse_args()
   633→
   634→    if not args.proxy_log.exists():
   635→        print(f"Error: {args.proxy_log} not found", file=sys.stderr)
   636→        sys.exit(1)
   637→
   638→    analysis = analyze_system_prompts(args.proxy_log)
   639→
   640→    if args.json:
   641→        out = asdict(analysis)
   642→        # Trim large fields
   643→        out.pop("sample_decomposition", None)
   644→        ct = out.pop("component_tracks", None)
   645→        if ct:
   646→            out["component_tracks"] = [
   647→                {
   648→                    "name": t["name"],
   649→                    "total_bytes_sent": sum(t["sizes"]),
   650→                    "turns_present": len(t["hashes"]),
   651→                    "is_static": len(set(t["hashes"])) <= 1,
   652→                    "change_count": sum(
   653→                        1 for i in range(1, len(t["hashes"]))
   654→                        if t["hashes"][i] != t["hashes"][i - 1]
   655→                    ),
   656→                }
   657→                for t in ct
   658→            ]
   659→        print(json.dumps(out, indent=2))
   660→    else:
   661→        print_analysis(analysis)
   662→
   663→
   664→if __name__ == "__main__":
   665→    main()
   666→

Context paging evaluation framework. Quechua for "to sweep, to clean."

Measures the effect of removing dead tool outputs from agentic LLM
context windows.

## Setup

```bash
uv sync
source .venv/bin/activate
```

Python 3.14. uv, not pip.

src/pichay/
    proxy.py      — MITM proxy between Claude Code and Anthropic API
    pager.py      — FIFO eviction engine for stale tool results
    eval.py       — analysis framework (per-turn metrics, cost estimates)
    __main__.py   — experiment runner CLI

1→"""Tests for pichay.replay — offline replay of context paging on proxy logs."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import json
     6→import sys
     7→from pathlib import Path
     8→
     9→import pytest
    10→
    11→from pichay.pager import PageEntry, PageStore
    12→from pichay.replay import (
    13→    ReplayTurn,
    14→    SessionReplay,
    15→    _apply_evictions,
    16→    main,
    17→    print_aggregate,
    18→    print_session_report,
    19→    replay_session,
    20→)
    21→
    22→
    23→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24→# Helpers
    25→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→
    27→def _write_jsonl(tmp_path: Path, name: str, records: list[dict]) -> Path:
    28→    """Write a list of dicts as JSONL and return the path."""
    29→    p = tmp_path / name
    30→    with open(p, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    31→        for rec in records:
    32→            f.write(json.dumps(rec) + "\n")
    33→    return p
    34→
    35→
    36→def _tool_use(tid: str, name: str, inp: dict) -> dict:
    37→    return {"type": "tool_use", "id": tid, "name": name, "input": inp}
    38→
    39→
    40→def _tool_result(tid: str, content: str | list, is_error: bool = False) -> dict:
    41→    d: dict = {"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": tid, "content": content}
    42→    if is_error:
    43→        d["is_error"] = True
    44→    return d
    45→
    46→
    47→def _req(messages_full: list[dict], ts: str = "2026-03-01T12:00:00Z") -> dict:
    48→    return {"type": "request", "timestamp": ts, "messages_full": messages_full}
    49→
    50→
    51→# Content large enough to exceed min_size=500 default.
    52→BIG = "A" * 800
    53→
    54→# Content too small to be evicted with default min_size=500.
    55→SMALL = "x" * 50
    56→
    57→
    58→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    59→# (1) Empty log file
    60→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    61→
    62→class TestEmptyLog:
    63→    def test_returns_zero_turns(self, tmp_path):
    64→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "empty.jsonl", [])
    65→        s = replay_session(p)
    66→        assert s.total_turns == 0
    67→        assert s.total_evictions == 0
    68→        assert s.total_bytes_saved == 0
    69→        assert s.total_bytes_original == 0
    70→        assert s.total_faults == 0
    71→        assert s.turns == []
    72→
    73→    def test_properties_safe_on_empty(self, tmp_path):
    74→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "empty.jsonl", [])
    75→        s = replay_session(p)
    76→        assert s.fault_rate == 0.0
    77→        assert s.reduction_pct == 0.0
    78→
    79→    def test_log_path_stored(self, tmp_path):
    80→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "empty.jsonl", [])
    81→        s = replay_session(p)
    82→        assert s.log_path == str(p)
    83→
    84→
    85→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    86→# (2) Log with no messages_full field (pre-replay logs)
    87→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    88→
    89→class TestNoMessagesFull:
    90→    def test_pre_replay_log_skipped(self, tmp_path):
    91→        """Older proxy logs that lack messages_full produce no turns."""
    92→        records = [
    93→            {
    94→                "type": "request",
    95→                "timestamp": "2026-03-01T12:00:00Z",
    96→                "model": "claude-opus-4-6",
    97→                "messages": {"tool_result_count": 5},
    98→            },
    99→            {
   100→                "type": "response",
   101→                "timestamp": "2026-03-01T12:00:01Z",
   102→                "usage": {"input_tokens": 1000},
   103→            },
   104→        ]
   105→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "old.jsonl", records)
   106→        s = replay_session(p)
   107→        assert s.total_turns == 0
   108→        assert s.turns == []
   109→
   110→    def test_non_request_records_ignored(self, tmp_path):
   111→        """Response, compaction, and other record types are not processed."""
   112→        records = [
   113→            {"type": "response", "timestamp": "T1", "messages_full": []},
   114→            {"type": "compaction", "timestamp": "T2"},
   115→            {"type": "page_faults", "timestamp": "T3"},
   116→        ]
   117→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "misc.jsonl", records)
   118→        s = replay_session(p)
   119→        assert s.total_turns == 0
   120→
   121→    def test_mixed_records_only_eligible_processed(self, tmp_path):
   122→        """Only request records WITH messages_full are counted as turns."""
   123→        records = [
   124→            {"type": "request", "timestamp": "T1"},  # no messages_full
   125→            {"type": "response", "timestamp": "T2"},
   126→            {
   127→                "type": "request",
   128→                "timestamp": "T3",
   129→                "messages_full": [
   130→                    {"role": "user", "content": "hello"},
   131→                ],
   132→            },
   133→        ]
   134→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "mixed.jsonl", records)
   135→        s = replay_session(p)
   136→        assert s.total_turns == 1
   137→        assert len(s.turns) == 1
   138→
   139→
   140→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   141→# (3) Single-turn session (too few turns for compaction)
   142→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   143→
   144→class TestSingleTurn:
   145→    def test_no_eviction_default_threshold(self, tmp_path):
   146→        """One API call with 2 user turns and default age_threshold=4
   147→        produces no evictions (max turns_from_end = 1 < 4)."""
   148→        messages = [
   149→            {"role": "user", "content": "read /foo.py"},
   150→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   151→                _tool_use("tu_1", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   152→            ]},
   153→            {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_1", BIG)]},
   154→        ]
   155→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "single.jsonl", [_req(messages)])
   156→        s = replay_session(p)
   157→
   158→        assert s.total_turns == 1
   159→        assert s.total_evictions == 0
   160→        assert s.total_faults == 0
   161→        assert len(s.turns) == 1
   162→        t = s.turns[0]
   163→        assert t.turn == 1
   164→        assert t.evictions == 0
   165→        assert t.faults == 0
   166→        assert t.bytes_saved == 0
   167→        assert t.bytes_original == t.bytes_compacted
   168→
   169→    def test_small_results_never_evicted(self, tmp_path):
   170→        """Even if old enough, results below min_size are kept."""
   171→        # 4 user turns with age_threshold=2: turn 1 has tfend=3 (string, skip),
   172→        # turn 2 has tfend=2 (eligible), but content is SMALL → skipped_small.
   173→        messages = [
   174→            {"role": "user", "content": "read"},
   175→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [

   208→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   209→# (4) Multi-turn session where tool results age past threshold
   210→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   211→
   212→def _multi_turn_request_1() -> dict:
   213→    """First API call: 4 user turns. With age_threshold=2, the tool_result
   214→    in user turn 2 (tu_1) is old enough to be evicted (turns_from_end=2)."""
   215→    messages = [
   216→        {"role": "user", "content": "read foo"},
   217→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   218→            _tool_use("tu_1", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   219→        ]},
   220→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_1", BIG)]},
   221→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "got it"}]},
   222→        {"role": "user", "content": "read bar"},
   223→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   224→            _tool_use("tu_2", "Read", {"file_path": "/bar.py"})
   225→        ]},
   226→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_2", BIG)]},
   227→    ]
   228→    return _req(messages, ts="2026-03-01T12:00:00Z")
   229→
   230→
   231→def _multi_turn_request_2() -> dict:
   232→    """Second API call: 6 user turns. tu_1 was evicted in request 1.
   233→    Now tu_2 (user turn 4, turns_from_end=2) also gets evicted."""
   234→    messages = [
   235→        {"role": "user", "content": "read foo"},
   236→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   237→            _tool_use("tu_1", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   238→        ]},
   239→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_1", BIG)]},
   240→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "got it"}]},
   241→        {"role": "user", "content": "read bar"},
   242→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   243→            _tool_use("tu_2", "Read", {"file_path": "/bar.py"})
   244→        ]},
   245→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_2", BIG)]},
   246→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "ok"}]},
   247→        {"role": "user", "content": "do stuff"},
   248→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   249→            _tool_use("tu_5", "Grep", {"pattern": "TODO", "path": "."})
   250→        ]},
   251→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_5", BIG)]},
   252→    ]
   253→    return _req(messages, ts="2026-03-01T12:01:00Z")
   254→
   255→
   256→class TestMultiTurnEviction:
   257→    def test_first_request_evicts_oldest(self, tmp_path):
   258→        """Request 1: 4 user turns, threshold=2, tu_1 evicted."""
   259→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "multi.jsonl", [_multi_turn_request_1()])
   260→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   261→
   262→        assert s.total_turns == 1
   263→        assert s.total_evictions == 1
   264→        t = s.turns[0]
   265→        assert t.evictions == 1
   266→        assert t.bytes_saved > 0
   267→
   268→    def test_cumulative_evictions_across_requests(self, tmp_path):
   269→        """Two requests: tu_1 evicted in req 1, tu_2 evicted in req 2."""
   270→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _multi_turn_request_2()]
   271→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "multi2.jsonl", records)
   272→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   273→
   274→        assert s.total_turns == 2
   275→        assert s.total_evictions == 2
   276→        assert s.turns[0].evictions == 1
   277→        assert s.turns[0].cumulative_evictions == 1
   278→        assert s.turns[1].cumulative_evictions == 2
   279→
   280→    def test_bytes_saved_increases(self, tmp_path):
   281→        """Total bytes saved grows with each eviction."""
   282→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _multi_turn_request_2()]
   283→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "multi3.jsonl", records)
   284→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   285→
   286→        assert s.total_bytes_saved > 0
   287→        # Each eviction replaces ~800 bytes of content with a short summary
   288→        assert s.turns[0].bytes_saved > 0
   289→        assert s.turns[1].bytes_saved > 0
   290→
   291→    def test_reduction_pct(self, tmp_path):
   292→        """Reduction percentage is correctly computed."""
   293→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _multi_turn_request_2()]
   294→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "multi4.jsonl", records)
   295→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   296→
   297→        # Session-level reduction
   298→        assert s.reduction_pct > 0.0
   299→        expected_pct = (s.total_bytes_saved / s.total_bytes_original) * 100
   300→        assert abs(s.reduction_pct - expected_pct) < 0.01
   301→
   302→    def test_apply_evictions_replaces_previously_evicted(self, tmp_path):
   303→        """Request 2 should see tu_1's content replaced by _apply_evictions
   304→        before compact_messages runs, since tu_1 was evicted in request 1."""
   305→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _multi_turn_request_2()]
   306→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "multi5.jsonl", records)
   307→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   308→
   309→        # Request 2: bytes_original is from the raw log (includes full tu_1
   310→        # content), but bytes_compacted reflects both _apply_evictions on tu_1
   311→        # AND the new compaction of tu_2.  So bytes_saved on request 2 should
   312→        # be larger than on request 1 (or at least comparable).
   313→        assert s.turns[1].bytes_saved > 0
   314→
   315→    def test_custom_thresholds(self, tmp_path):
   316→        """Higher age_threshold means fewer evictions."""
   317→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1()]
   318→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "threshold.jsonl", records)
   319→
   320→        # With age_threshold=2: tu_1 evicted (4 user turns, tfend=2)
   321→        s2 = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   322→        assert s2.total_evictions == 1
   323→
   324→        # With age_threshold=4: tu_1 NOT evicted (tfend=2 < 4)
   325→        s4 = replay_session(p, age_threshold=4)
   326→        assert s4.total_evictions == 0
   327→
   328→    def test_custom_min_size(self, tmp_path):
   329→        """Higher min_size prevents smaller results from being evicted."""
   330→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1()]
   331→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "minsize.jsonl", records)
   332→
   333→        # BIG is 800 bytes.  min_size=500 → evicted.
   334→        s_lo = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2, min_size=500)
   335→        assert s_lo.total_evictions == 1
   336→
   337→        # min_size=1000 → BIG (800) is too small → not evicted.
   338→        s_hi = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2, min_size=1000)
   339→        assert s_hi.total_evictions == 0

   342→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   343→# (5) Page fault detection when model re-requests evicted content
   344→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   345→
   346→def _fault_request_2() -> dict:
   347→    """Second request that includes a re-read of /foo.py (tu_3) after
   348→    tu_1 (Read /foo.py) was evicted in request 1. tu_3 is a page fault."""
   349→    messages = [
   350→        {"role": "user", "content": "read foo"},
   351→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   352→            _tool_use("tu_1", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   353→        ]},
   354→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_1", BIG)]},
   355→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "got it"}]},
   356→        {"role": "user", "content": "read bar"},
   357→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   358→            _tool_use("tu_2", "Read", {"file_path": "/bar.py"})
   359→        ]},
   360→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_2", BIG)]},
   361→        # The model re-requests /foo.py (page fault)
   362→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   363→            _tool_use("tu_3", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   364→        ]},
   365→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_3", "re-read content")]},
   366→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "done"}]},
   367→        {"role": "user", "content": "continue"},
   368→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   369→            _tool_use("tu_6", "Grep", {"pattern": "TODO", "path": "."})
   370→        ]},
   371→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_6", BIG)]},
   372→    ]
   373→    return _req(messages, ts="2026-03-01T12:01:00Z")
   374→
   375→
   376→class TestPageFaultDetection:
   377→    def test_fault_detected(self, tmp_path):
   378→        """A re-read of /foo.py after tu_1 eviction is a page fault."""
   379→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _fault_request_2()]
   380→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "fault.jsonl", records)
   381→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   382→
   383→        # Request 1: tu_1 evicted, no faults.
   384→        assert s.turns[0].faults == 0
   385→        # Request 2: tu_3 re-reads /foo.py → fault detected.
   386→        assert s.turns[1].faults == 1
   387→        assert s.total_faults == 1
   388→
   389→    def test_fault_rate(self, tmp_path):
   390→        """fault_rate = total_faults / total_evictions."""
   391→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _fault_request_2()]
   392→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "faultrate.jsonl", records)
   393→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   394→
   395→        assert s.total_evictions > 0
   396→        expected = s.total_faults / s.total_evictions
   397→        assert abs(s.fault_rate - expected) < 1e-9
   398→
   399→    def test_cumulative_faults(self, tmp_path):
   400→        """cumulative_faults tracks running total across turns."""
   401→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _fault_request_2()]
   402→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "cumfault.jsonl", records)
   403→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   404→
   405→        assert s.turns[0].cumulative_faults == 0
   406→        assert s.turns[1].cumulative_faults == 1
   407→
   408→    def test_original_tool_use_not_counted_as_fault(self, tmp_path):
   409→        """The original tool_use (tu_1) that produced the evicted result
   410→        should NOT be counted as a fault even though it matches."""
   411→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1()]
   412→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "nofault.jsonl", records)
   413→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   414→
   415→        # tu_1 is in the assistant message AND was evicted, but it's the
   416→        # original call — not a re-request. No fault.
   417→        assert s.total_faults == 0
   418→
   419→    def test_no_double_counting_across_requests(self, tmp_path):
   420→        """The same fault tool_use_id appearing in multiple request records
   421→        should only be counted once."""
   422→        req1 = _multi_turn_request_1()
   423→        req2 = _fault_request_2()
   424→        # Request 3 is a superset of request 2 (session has grown).
   425→        # tu_3 (the fault) is still in the messages.
   426→        msgs3 = json.loads(json.dumps(req2["messages_full"]))
   427→        msgs3.extend([
   428→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "more"}]},
   429→            {"role": "user", "content": "keep going"},
   430→        ])
   431→        req3 = _req(msgs3, ts="2026-03-01T12:02:00Z")
   432→
   433→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "dedup.jsonl", [req1, req2, req3])
   434→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   435→
   436→        # tu_3 appears in both req2 and req3, but only counted once.
   437→        assert s.total_faults == 1
   438→

1→"""Context window pager — evicts stale tool results from the messages array.
     2→
     3→This is the intervention layer for the phase 1 context utilization experiment.
     4→It sits between Claude Code and Anthropic's API (inside the proxy) and replaces
     5→old, large tool results with compact summaries. The originals are stored in a
     6→page file for logging and analysis.
     7→
     8→Design decisions:
     9→- FIFO eviction: oldest results first (data shows Q1 results have 0.896
    10→  amplification ratio — evicting them captures the most benefit)
    11→- No recall tool injection: if the model needs evicted content, it already
    12→  knows how to re-issue the tool call (Read, Grep, etc.). The "page fault"
    13→  is just a new tool call. PDP-11 overlays, not virtual memory.
    14→- Error results are never evicted (the model needs those for debugging)
    15→- Small results (<min_size bytes) aren't worth compacting
    16→
    17→Metrics for success (from Tony):
    18→- Lower token consumption
    19→- Better quality output (fewer tokens → LLMs work better)
    20→- Faster responses
    21→- Slower consumption of the context window
    22→"""
    23→
    24→from __future__ import annotations
    25→
    26→import json
    27→import time
    28→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    29→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    30→from pathlib import Path
    31→
    32→
    33→@dataclass
    34→class PageEntry:
    35→    """A single evicted tool result."""
    36→
    37→    tool_use_id: str
    38→    tool_name: str
    39→    tool_input: dict
    40→    original_content: str | list
    41→    original_size: int
    42→    summary: str
    43→    evicted_at: float  # time.monotonic()
    44→    turn_index: int
    45→    turns_from_end: int
    46→
    47→
    48→@dataclass
    49→class PageFault:
    50→    """A tool call that re-requests evicted content."""
    51→
    52→    tool_use_id: str
    53→    tool_name: str
    54→    tool_input: dict
    55→    original_eviction: PageEntry
    56→    detected_at: float  # time.monotonic()
    57→
    58→
    59→@dataclass
    60→class CompactionStats:
    61→    """What happened during a single compaction pass."""
    62→
    63→    total_tool_results: int = 0
    64→    evicted_count: int = 0
    65→    bytes_before: int = 0
    66→    bytes_after: int = 0
    67→    skipped_small: int = 0
    68→    skipped_recent: int = 0
    69→    skipped_error: int = 0
    70→
    71→    @property
    72→    def bytes_saved(self) -> int:
    73→        return self.bytes_before - self.bytes_after
    74→
    75→    @property
    76→    def reduction_pct(self) -> float:
    77→        if self.bytes_before == 0:
    78→            return 0.0
    79→        return (self.bytes_saved / self.bytes_before) * 100
    80→
    81→
    82→class PageStore:
    83→    """Stores evicted tool result content and detects page faults.
    84→
    85→    In-memory for now. The page file is for analysis, not production.
    86→
    87→    A page fault is when the model re-issues a tool call for content
    88→    that was evicted. This is the key observability signal: it tells
    89→    us whether eviction was wrong (costly) or right (content was dead).
    90→    """
    91→
    92→    def __init__(self, log_path: Path | None = None):
    93→        self.pages: dict[str, PageEntry] = {}
    94→        self.log_path = log_path
    95→        self.cumulative_bytes_saved: int = 0
    96→        self.cumulative_evictions: int = 0
    97→        self.faults: list[PageFault] = []
    98→        # Index for fault detection: (tool_name, key) → PageEntry
    99→        # Key is the identifying parameter (file_path for Read, etc.)
   100→        self._eviction_index: dict[tuple[str, str], PageEntry] = {}
   101→
   102→    def store(self, entry: PageEntry) -> None:
   103→        self.pages[entry.tool_use_id] = entry
   104→        self.cumulative_bytes_saved += entry.original_size - len(
   105→            entry.summary.encode("utf-8")
   106→        )
   107→        self.cumulative_evictions += 1
   108→
   109→        # Index for fault detection
   110→        key = _eviction_key(entry.tool_name, entry.tool_input)
   111→        if key is not None:
   112→            self._eviction_index[(entry.tool_name, key)] = entry
   113→
   114→        if self.log_path is not None:
   115→            record = {
   116→                "type": "eviction",
   117→                "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   118→                "tool_use_id": entry.tool_use_id,
   119→                "tool_name": entry.tool_name,
   120→                "original_size": entry.original_size,
   121→                "summary_size": len(entry.summary.encode("utf-8")),
   122→                "turn_index": entry.turn_index,
   123→                "turns_from_end": entry.turns_from_end,
   124→            }
   125→            with open(self.log_path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   126→                f.write(json.dumps(record) + "\n")
   127→
   128→    def detect_faults(self, messages: list[dict]) -> list[PageFault]:
   129→        """Scan recent tool_use blocks for re-requests of evicted content.
   130→
   131→        Looks at tool_use blocks in assistant messages and checks if any
   132→        match evicted pages. A match means the model needed content we
   133→        took away — a page fault.
   134→        """
   135→        new_faults = []
   136→        for msg in messages:
   137→            if msg.get("role") != "assistant":
   138→                continue
   139→            content = msg.get("content", [])
   140→            if not isinstance(content, list):
   141→                continue
   142→            for block in content:
   143→                if not isinstance(block, dict):
   144→                    continue
   145→                if block.get("type") != "tool_use":
   146→                    continue
   147→
   148→                tool_name = block.get("name", "")
   149→                tool_input = block.get("input", {})
   150→                tool_use_id = block.get("id", "")
   151→
   152→                # Skip the original tool_use that produced the
   153→                # evicted result — that's not a fault, it's history
   154→                if tool_use_id in self.pages:
   155→                    continue
   156→
   157→                key = _eviction_key(tool_name, tool_input)
   158→                if key is None:
   159→                    continue
   160→
   161→                evicted = self._eviction_index.get((tool_name, key))
   162→                if evicted is None:
   163→                    continue
   164→
   165→                # Don't double-count: check if this tool_use_id
   166→                # was already recorded as a fault
   167→                if any(f.tool_use_id == tool_use_id for f in self.faults):
   168→                    continue
   169→
   170→                fault = PageFault(
   171→                    tool_use_id=tool_use_id,
   172→                    tool_name=tool_name,
   173→                    tool_input=tool_input,
   174→                    original_eviction=evicted,
   175→                    detected_at=time.monotonic(),
   176→                )
   177→                new_faults.append(fault)
   178→                self.faults.append(fault)
   179→
   180→                if self.log_path is not None:
   181→                    record = {
   182→                        "type": "page_fault",
   183→                        "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   184→                        "tool_name": tool_name,
   185→                        "eviction_key": key,
   186→                        "original_tool_use_id": evicted.tool_use_id,
   187→                        "original_size": evicted.original_size,
   188→                        "original_turn": evicted.turn_index,
   189→                    }
   190→                    with open(self.log_path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   191→                        f.write(json.dumps(record) + "\n")
   192→
   193→        return new_faults
   194→
   195→    def retrieve(self, tool_use_id: str) -> PageEntry | None:
   196→        return self.pages.get(tool_use_id)
   197→
   198→    def summary(self) -> dict:
   199→        """Current state for the health endpoint."""
   200→        return {
   201→            "total_evictions": self.cumulative_evictions,
   202→            "total_bytes_saved": self.cumulative_bytes_saved,
   203→            "total_page_faults": len(self.faults),
   204→            "fault_rate": (
   205→                len(self.faults) / self.cumulative_evictions
   206→                if self.cumulative_evictions > 0
   207→                else 0.0
   208→            ),
   209→            "pages_in_store": len(self.pages),
   210→            "faults_by_tool": _count_by(
   211→                self.faults, lambda f: f.tool_name
   212→            ),
   213→            "evictions_by_tool": _count_by(
   214→                list(self.pages.values()), lambda p: p.tool_name
   215→            ),
   216→        }
   217→
   218→
   219→def _count_by(items: list, key_fn) -> dict[str, int]:
   220→    counts: dict[str, int] = {}
   221→    for item in items:
   222→        k = key_fn(item)
   223→        counts[k] = counts.get(k, 0) + 1
   224→    return counts
   225→
   226→
   227→def _eviction_key(tool_name: str, tool_input: dict) -> str | None:
   228→    """Extract the identifying key for a tool call, for fault matching.
   229→
   230→    Returns None for tools where re-invocation isn't meaningful
   231→    (e.g., Agent results can't be re-requested by input match).
   232→    """
   233→    if tool_name == "Read":
   234→        return tool_input.get("file_path")
   235→    elif tool_name == "Grep":
   236→        # pattern + path is the identity
   237→        pattern = tool_input.get("pattern", "")
   238→        path = tool_input.get("path", ".")
   239→        return f"{pattern}@{path}"
   240→    elif tool_name == "Glob":
   241→        return tool_input.get("pattern")
   242→    elif tool_name == "Bash":
   243→        return tool_input.get("command")
   244→    elif tool_name == "WebFetch":
   245→        return tool_input.get("url")
   246→    elif tool_name == "WebSearch":
   247→        return tool_input.get("query")
   248→    return None
   249→
   250→
   251→def _content_size(content: str | list | None) -> int:
   252→    """Measure content size in bytes."""
   253→    if content is None:
   254→        return 0
   255→    if isinstance(content, str):
   256→        return len(content.encode("utf-8"))
   257→    return len(json.dumps(content).encode("utf-8"))
   258→
   259→
   260→def _build_tool_use_index(messages: list[dict]) -> dict[str, dict]:
   261→    """Map tool_use_id → {name, input} from assistant messages."""
   262→    index = {}
   263→    for msg in messages:
   264→        if msg.get("role") != "assistant":
   265→            continue
   266→        content = msg.get("content", [])
   267→        if not isinstance(content, list):
   268→            continue
   269→        for block in content:
   270→            if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_use":
   271→                index[block["id"]] = {
   272→                    "name": block.get("name", "unknown"),
   273→                    "input": block.get("input", {}),
   274→                }
   275→    return index
   276→
   277→
   278→def _make_summary(tool_name: str, tool_input: dict, original_size: int,
   279→                  original_content: str | list | None = None) -> str:
   280→    """Generate a compact summary for an evicted tool result.
   281→
   282→    The summary tells the model what WAS here and how to get it back
   283→    (re-issue the same tool call). No magic — just information.
   284→    """
   285→    size_str = f"{original_size:,}"
   286→
   287→    if tool_name == "Read":
   288→        path = tool_input.get("file_path", "unknown")
   289→        line_count = ""
   290→        if isinstance(original_content, str):
   291→            lines = original_content.count("\n")
   292→            line_count = f", {lines} lines"
   293→        return (
   294→            f"[Paged out: Read {path} ({size_str} bytes{line_count}). "
   295→            f"Re-read the file if you need its content.]"
   296→        )
   297→
   298→    elif tool_name == "Grep":
   299→        pattern = tool_input.get("pattern", "?")
   300→        path = tool_input.get("path", ".")
   301→        mode = tool_input.get("output_mode", "files_with_matches")
   302→        # Count result lines if string content
   303→        match_info = ""
   304→        if isinstance(original_content, str):
   305→            result_lines = len(original_content.strip().splitlines())
   306→            match_info = f", {result_lines} result lines"
   307→        return (
   308→            f"[Paged out: Grep '{pattern}' in {path} "
   309→            f"(mode={mode}{match_info}, {size_str} bytes). "
   310→            f"Re-run the search if you need results.]"
   311→        )
   312→
   313→    elif tool_name == "Glob":
   314→        pattern = tool_input.get("pattern", "?")
   315→        match_info = ""
   316→        if isinstance(original_content, str):
   317→            matches = len(original_content.strip().splitlines())
   318→            match_info = f", {matches} matches"
   319→        return (
   320→            f"[Paged out: Glob '{pattern}'{match_info} ({size_str} bytes). "
   321→            f"Re-run if you need the file list.]"
   322→        )
   323→
   324→    elif tool_name == "Bash":
   325→        cmd = tool_input.get("command", "?")
   326→        # Truncate long commands
   327→        if len(cmd) > 120:
   328→            cmd = cmd[:117] + "..."
   329→        return (
   330→            f"[Paged out: Bash `{cmd}` ({size_str} bytes). "
   331→            f"Re-run the command if you need its output.]"
   332→        )
   333→
   334→    elif tool_name == "WebFetch":
   335→        url = tool_input.get("url", "?")
   336→        return (
   337→            f"[Paged out: WebFetch {url} ({size_str} bytes). "
   338→            f"Re-fetch if you need the content.]"
   339→        )
   340→
   341→    elif tool_name == "WebSearch":
   342→        query = tool_input.get("query", "?")
   343→        return (
   344→            f"[Paged out: WebSearch '{query}' ({size_str} bytes). "
   345→            f"Re-search if you need results.]"
   346→        )
   347→
   348→    elif tool_name in ("Agent", "TaskOutput"):
   349→        return (
   350→            f"[Paged out: {tool_name} result ({size_str} bytes). "
   351→            f"Content was consumed when originally returned.]"
   352→        )
   353→
   354→    else:
   355→        return (
   356→            f"[Paged out: {tool_name} ({size_str} bytes). "
   357→            f"Re-invoke the tool if you need this content.]"
   358→        )
   359→
   360→
   361→def compact_messages(
   362→    messages: list[dict],
   363→    age_threshold: int = 4,
   364→    min_size: int = 500,
   365→    page_store: PageStore | None = None,
   366→) -> CompactionStats:
   367→    """Replace old, large tool results with compact summaries.
   368→
   369→    Mutates the messages list in place. Returns stats about what was done.
   370→
   371→    Args:
   372→        messages: The messages array from the API request body.
   373→        age_threshold: Results older than this many user-turns from the
   374→            end get evicted. Default 4.
   375→        min_size: Results smaller than this (bytes) are kept as-is.
   376→            Not worth compacting a 22-byte Edit confirmation. Default 500.
   377→        page_store: If provided, evicted content is stored here.
   378→    """
   379→    stats = CompactionStats()
   380→    tool_use_index = _build_tool_use_index(messages)
   381→
   382→    # Count user turns (each user message is a "turn")
   383→    user_turn_indices: list[int] = []
   384→    for i, msg in enumerate(messages):
   385→        if msg.get("role") == "user":
   386→            user_turn_indices.append(i)
   387→
   388→    total_user_turns = len(user_turn_indices)
   389→
   390→    # Walk through messages and compact old tool results
   391→    current_user_turn = 0
   392→    for msg_idx, msg in enumerate(messages):
   393→        if msg.get("role") != "user":
   394→            continue
   395→
   396→        current_user_turn += 1
   397→        turns_from_end = total_user_turns - current_user_turn
   398→
   399→        content = msg.get("content", [])
   400→        if not isinstance(content, list):
   401→            continue
   402→
   403→        for block_idx, block in enumerate(content):
   404→            if not isinstance(block, dict):
   405→                continue
   406→            if block.get("type") != "tool_result":
   407→                continue
   408→
   409→            stats.total_tool_results += 1
   410→            original_content = block.get("content", "")
   411→            original_size = _content_size(original_content)
   412→
   413→            # Skip errors — model needs those
   414→            if block.get("is_error", False):
   415→                stats.skipped_error += 1
   416→                continue
   417→
   418→            # Skip recent results
   419→            if turns_from_end < age_threshold:
   420→                stats.skipped_recent += 1
   421→                stats.bytes_before += original_size
   422→                stats.bytes_after += original_size
   423→                continue
   424→
   425→            # Skip small results
   426→            if original_size < min_size:
   427→                stats.skipped_small += 1
   428→                stats.bytes_before += original_size
   429→                stats.bytes_after += original_size
   430→                continue
   431→
   432→            # This result gets evicted
   433→            tool_use_id = block.get("tool_use_id", "")
   434→            tool_info = tool_use_index.get(tool_use_id, {})
   435→            tool_name = tool_info.get("name", "unknown")
   436→            tool_input = tool_info.get("input", {})
   437→
   438→            summary = _make_summary(
   439→                tool_name, tool_input, original_size, original_content
   440→            )
   441→
   442→            # Store the original
   443→            if page_store is not None:
   444→                entry = PageEntry(
   445→                    tool_use_id=tool_use_id,
   446→                    tool_name=tool_name,
   447→                    tool_input=tool_input,
   448→                    original_content=original_content,
   449→                    original_size=original_size,
   450→                    summary=summary,
   451→                    evicted_at=time.monotonic(),
   452→                    turn_index=current_user_turn,
   453→                    turns_from_end=turns_from_end,
   454→                )
   455→                page_store.store(entry)
   456→
   457→            # Replace the content
   458→            content[block_idx] = {**block, "content": summary}
   459→
   460→            stats.evicted_count += 1
   461→            stats.bytes_before += original_size
   462→            stats.bytes_after += len(summary.encode("utf-8"))
   463→
   464→    return stats
   465→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""Logging proxy for Claude API calls, with optional context paging.
     3→
     4→Two modes:
     5→    Observe (default):  Logs request/response metrics. Pure observation.
     6→    Compact (--compact): Also evicts stale tool results from the messages
     7→        array before forwarding, replacing them with compact summaries.
     8→
     9→Usage:
    10→    # Observation only
    11→    python -m pichay.proxy [--port 0] [--log-dir logs]
    12→
    13→    # With context paging
    14→    python -m pichay.proxy --compact [--age-threshold 4] [--min-size 500]
    15→
    16→    # Point Claude Code at it
    17→    ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:<port> claude
    18→
    19→Port 0 (default) picks a random free port to avoid collisions.
    20→"""
    21→
    22→from __future__ import annotations
    23→
    24→import argparse
    25→import json
    26→import os
    27→import socket
    28→import sys
    29→import time
    30→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    31→from pathlib import Path
    32→
    33→import httpx
    34→from flask import Flask, Response, request
    35→
    36→from pichay.pager import PageStore, compact_messages
    37→
    38→ANTHROPIC_API_BASE = "https://api.anthropic.com"
    39→
    40→
    41→def find_free_port() -> int:
    42→    """Find a free port by binding to port 0 and reading the assignment."""
    43→    with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET6, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
    44→        s.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1)
    45→        try:
    46→            # Try IPv6 loopback first (reduces collision space)
    47→            s.bind(("::1", 0))
    48→        except OSError:
    49→            # Fall back to IPv4
    50→            s.close()
    51→            with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s4:
    52→                s4.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1)
    53→                s4.bind(("127.0.0.1", 0))
    54→                return s4.getsockname()[1]
    55→        return s.getsockname()[1]
    56→
    57→
    58→def create_app(
    59→    log_dir: Path,
    60→    compact: bool = False,
    61→    trim: bool = False,
    62→    age_threshold: int = 4,
    63→    min_size: int = 500,
    64→) -> Flask:
    65→    """Create the proxy Flask app."""
    66→    app = Flask(__name__)
    67→    log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    68→
    69→    # One log file per proxy session
    70→    session_start = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    71→    log_file = log_dir / f"proxy_{session_start}.jsonl"
    72→
    73→    # Pager state (lives for the proxy session)
    74→    page_store = None
    75→    if compact:
    76→        page_log = log_dir / f"pages_{session_start}.jsonl"
    77→        page_store = PageStore(log_path=page_log)
    78→        print(
    79→            f"Compact mode: age_threshold={age_threshold}, "
    80→            f"min_size={min_size}",
    81→            file=sys.stderr,
    82→        )
    83→        print(f"Page log: {page_log}", file=sys.stderr)
    84→
    85→    # Trimmer state (lives for the proxy session)
    86→    trimmer = None
    87→    if trim:
    88→        from pichay.trimmer import SystemPromptTrimmer
    89→        # log_record defined below; late-bind via lambda
    90→        trimmer = SystemPromptTrimmer()
    91→        print(
    92→            "Trim mode: tool stubs + skill dedup + static tracking",
    93→            file=sys.stderr,
    94→        )
    95→
    96→    # Persistent HTTP client for forwarding
    97→    client = httpx.Client(
    98→        base_url=ANTHROPIC_API_BASE,
    99→        timeout=httpx.Timeout(300.0, connect=30.0),
   100→    )
   101→
   102→    def log_record(record: dict) -> None:
   103→        """Append a record to the log file."""
   104→        with open(log_file, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   105→            f.write(json.dumps(record, default=str) + "\n")
   106→
   107→    # Wire up trimmer logging now that log_record exists
   108→    if trimmer is not None:
   109→        trimmer.log_fn = log_record
   110→
   111→    def measure_system_prompt(body: dict) -> dict:
   112→        """Extract system prompt metrics."""
   113→        system = body.get("system", "")
   114→        if isinstance(system, str):
   115→            return {
   116→                "system_prompt_bytes": len(system.encode("utf-8")),
   117→                "system_prompt_type": "string",
   118→                "system_prompt_preview": system[:200],
   119→            }
   120→        elif isinstance(system, list):
   121→            total_bytes = sum(
   122→                len(json.dumps(block).encode("utf-8")) for block in system
   123→            )
   124→            block_types = [
   125→                block.get("type", "unknown")
   126→                for block in system
   127→                if isinstance(block, dict)
   128→            ]
   129→            return {
   130→                "system_prompt_bytes": total_bytes,
   131→                "system_prompt_type": "blocks",
   132→                "system_prompt_block_count": len(system),
   133→                "system_prompt_block_types": block_types,
   134→                "system_prompt_preview": json.dumps(system[0])[:200]
   135→                if system
   136→                else "",
   137→            }
   138→        return {"system_prompt_bytes": 0, "system_prompt_type": "absent"}
   139→
   140→    def measure_messages(body: dict) -> dict:
   141→        """Extract message array metrics without storing full content."""
   142→        messages = body.get("messages", [])
   143→        metrics = {
   144→            "message_count": len(messages),
   145→            "messages_total_bytes": len(
   146→                json.dumps(messages).encode("utf-8")
   147→            ),
   148→            "role_counts": {},
   149→            "tool_result_count": 0,
   150→            "tool_result_bytes": 0,
   151→            "tool_use_count": 0,
   152→            "text_bytes": 0,
   153→            "thinking_bytes": 0,
   154→        }
   155→
   156→        for msg in messages:
   157→            role = msg.get("role", "unknown")
   158→            metrics["role_counts"][role] = (
   159→                metrics["role_counts"].get(role, 0) + 1
   160→            )
   161→
   162→            content = msg.get("content", "")
   163→            if isinstance(content, str):
   164→                metrics["text_bytes"] += len(content.encode("utf-8"))
   165→            elif isinstance(content, list):
   166→                for block in content:
   167→                    if not isinstance(block, dict):
   168→                        continue
   169→                    block_type = block.get("type", "")
   170→                    if block_type == "tool_result":
   171→                        metrics["tool_result_count"] += 1
   172→                        result_content = block.get("content", "")
   173→                        if isinstance(result_content, str):
   174→                            metrics["tool_result_bytes"] += len(
   175→                                result_content.encode("utf-8")
   176→                            )
   177→                        else:
   178→                            metrics["tool_result_bytes"] += len(
   179→                                json.dumps(result_content).encode("utf-8")
   180→                            )
   181→                    elif block_type == "tool_use":
   182→                        metrics["tool_use_count"] += 1
   183→                    elif block_type == "text":
   184→                        metrics["text_bytes"] += len(
   185→                            block.get("text", "").encode("utf-8")
   186→                        )
   187→                    elif block_type == "thinking":
   188→                        metrics["thinking_bytes"] += len(
   189→                            block.get("thinking", "").encode("utf-8")
   190→                        )
   191→
   192→        return metrics
   193→
   194→    @app.route("/v1/messages", methods=["POST"])
   195→    def proxy_messages():
   196→        """Proxy the messages endpoint with full logging."""
   197→        request_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   198→        body = request.get_json(force=True)
   199→
   200→        # Measure without storing full content
   201→        system_metrics = measure_system_prompt(body)
   202→        message_metrics = measure_messages(body)
   203→
   204→        request_record = {
   205→            "type": "request",
   206→            "timestamp": request_time.isoformat(),
   207→            "model": body.get("model", "unknown"),
   208→            "max_tokens": body.get("max_tokens"),
   209→            "stream": body.get("stream", False),
   210→            "system": system_metrics,
   211→            "messages": message_metrics,
   212→            "total_request_bytes": len(
   213→                json.dumps(body).encode("utf-8")
   214→            ),
   215→        }
   216→
   217→        if body.get("system"):
   218→            request_record["system_prompt_full"] = body["system"]
   219→
   220→        # Store full messages for offline replay
   221→        request_record["messages_full"] = body.get("messages", [])
   222→
   223→        log_record(request_record)
   224→
   225→        # --- System prompt trimming (if enabled) ---
   226→        if trim and trimmer is not None:
   227→            trim_result = trimmer.trim(body)
   228→            if trim_result.total_bytes_saved > 0:
   229→                print(
   230→                    f"  Trimmed: {trim_result.tools.stubbed_tools} stubs, "
   231→                    f"{trim_result.skills.duplicates_removed} skill dupes, "
   232→                    f"saved {trim_result.total_bytes_saved:,} bytes",
   233→                    file=sys.stderr,
   234→                )
   235→
   236→        # --- Temperature override (if configured) ---
   237→        # Extended thinking requires temperature=1; skip override when thinking is active.
   238→        # Broad check: any truthy "thinking" field means thinking is requested.
   239→        thinking_enabled = bool(body.get("thinking"))
   240→        if app.config.get("temperature_override") is not None and not thinking_enabled:
   241→            body["temperature"] = app.config["temperature_override"]
   242→        elif app.config.get("temperature_override") is not None and thinking_enabled:
   243→            print(
   244→                f"  [proxy] Skipping temperature override "
   245→                f"(thinking enabled: {body.get('thinking')})",
   246→                file=sys.stderr,
   247→            )
   248→
   249→        # --- Context paging (if enabled) ---
   250→        if compact and page_store is not None:
   251→            messages = body.get("messages", [])
   252→
   253→            # Detect page faults BEFORE compaction
   254→            faults = page_store.detect_faults(messages)
   255→            if faults:
   256→                for fault in faults:
   257→                    print(
   258→                        f"  PAGE FAULT: {fault.tool_name} "
   259→                        f"re-requested (evicted from turn "
   260→                        f"{fault.original_eviction.turn_index}, "
   261→                        f"{fault.original_eviction.original_size:,} bytes)",
   262→                        file=sys.stderr,
   263→                    )
   264→                log_record({
   265→                    "type": "page_faults",
   266→                    "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   267→                    "count": len(faults),
   268→                    "faults": [
   269→                        {
   270→                            "tool_name": f.tool_name,
   271→                            "original_turn": f.original_eviction.turn_index,
   272→                            "original_size": f.original_eviction.original_size,
   273→                        }
   274→                        for f in faults
   275→                    ],
   276→                    "cumulative_faults": len(page_store.faults),
   277→                    "cumulative_evictions": page_store.cumulative_evictions,
   278→                    "fault_rate": (
   279→                        len(page_store.faults)
   280→                        / page_store.cumulative_evictions
   281→                        if page_store.cumulative_evictions > 0
   282→                        else 0.0
   283→                    ),
   284→                })
   285→
   286→            # Compact
   287→            stats = compact_messages(
   288→                messages,
   289→                age_threshold=age_threshold,
   290→                min_size=min_size,
   291→                page_store=page_store,
   292→            )
   293→            if stats.evicted_count > 0:
   294→                post_metrics = measure_messages(body)
   295→                compaction_record = {
   296→                    "type": "compaction",
   297→                    "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   298→                    "evicted": stats.evicted_count,
   299→                    "total_tool_results": stats.total_tool_results,
   300→                    "bytes_before": stats.bytes_before,
   301→                    "bytes_after": stats.bytes_after,
   302→                    "bytes_saved": stats.bytes_saved,
   303→                    "reduction_pct": round(stats.reduction_pct, 1),
   304→                    "skipped_small": stats.skipped_small,
   305→                    "skipped_recent": stats.skipped_recent,
   306→                    "skipped_error": stats.skipped_error,
   307→                    "cumulative_evictions": page_store.cumulative_evictions,
   308→                    "cumulative_bytes_saved": page_store.cumulative_bytes_saved,
   309→                    "cumulative_faults": len(page_store.faults),
   310→                    "fault_rate": round(
   311→                        len(page_store.faults)
   312→                        / page_store.cumulative_evictions
   313→                        * 100,
   314→                        1,
   315→                    )
   316→                    if page_store.cumulative_evictions > 0
   317→                    else 0.0,
   318→                    "messages_bytes_before": message_metrics[
   319→                        "messages_total_bytes"
   320→                    ],
   321→                    "messages_bytes_after": post_metrics[
   322→                        "messages_total_bytes"
   323→                    ],
   324→                }
   325→                log_record(compaction_record)
   326→                print(
   327→                    f"  Compacted: {stats.evicted_count} results, "
   328→                    f"saved {stats.bytes_saved:,} bytes "
   329→                    f"({stats.reduction_pct:.0f}%), "
   330→                    f"cumulative {page_store.cumulative_bytes_saved:,}, "
   331→                    f"faults {len(page_store.faults)}"
   332→                    f"/{page_store.cumulative_evictions}",
   333→                    file=sys.stderr,
   334→                )
   335→
   336→        # Forward to Anthropic
   337→        headers = dict(request.headers)
   338→        for h in ["Host", "Content-Length", "Transfer-Encoding"]:
   339→            headers.pop(h, None)
   340→
   341→        upstream_path = "/v1/messages"
   342→        if request.query_string:
   343→            upstream_path += "?" + request.query_string.decode("utf-8")
   344→
   345→        if body.get("stream", False):
   346→            return _proxy_streaming(body, headers, request_time, upstream_path)
   347→        else:
   348→            return _proxy_direct(body, headers, request_time, upstream_path)
   349→
   350→    def _strip_response_headers(raw_headers):
   351→        skip = {
   352→            "transfer-encoding",
   353→            "content-length",
   354→            "content-encoding",
   355→            "connection",
   356→            "keep-alive",
   357→        }
   358→        return {
   359→            k: v
   360→            for k, v in raw_headers.items()
   361→            if k.lower() not in skip
   362→        }
   363→
   364→    def _proxy_direct(body, headers, request_time, upstream_path):
   365→        try:
   366→            resp = client.post(
   367→                upstream_path, json=body, headers=headers,
   368→            )
   369→            response_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   370→            try:
   371→                resp_body = resp.json()
   372→                usage = resp_body.get("usage", {})
   373→                log_record({
   374→                    "type": "response",
   375→                    "timestamp": response_time.isoformat(),
   376→                    "duration_ms": int(
   377→                        (response_time - request_time).total_seconds() * 1000
   378→                    ),
   379→                    "status_code": resp.status_code,
   380→                    "usage": usage,
   381→                    "stop_reason": resp_body.get("stop_reason"),
   382→                })
   383→            except Exception:
   384→                log_record({
   385→                    "type": "response_error",
   386→                    "timestamp": response_time.isoformat(),
   387→                    "status_code": resp.status_code,
   388→                })
   389→            return Response(
   390→                resp.content,
   391→                status=resp.status_code,
   392→                headers=_strip_response_headers(resp.headers),
   393→            )
   394→        except Exception as e:
   395→            log_record({
   396→                "type": "proxy_error",
   397→                "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   398→                "error": str(e),
   399→            })
   400→            return Response(
   401→                json.dumps({"error": str(e)}),
   402→                status=502,
   403→                content_type="application/json",
   404→            )
   405→
   406→    def _proxy_streaming(body, headers, request_time, upstream_path):
   407→        try:
   408→            resp = client.send(
   409→                client.build_request(
   410→                    "POST", upstream_path, json=body, headers=headers,
   411→                ),
   412→                stream=True,
   413→            )
   414→            response_headers = _strip_response_headers(resp.headers)
   415→
   416→            def generate():
   417→                first_byte_time = None
   418→                chunks_collected = []
   419→                try:
   420→                    for chunk in resp.iter_bytes():
   421→                        if first_byte_time is None:
   422→                            first_byte_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   423→                        chunks_collected.append(chunk)
   424→                        yield chunk
   425→                finally:
   426→                    resp.close()
   427→                    response_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   428→                    full_response = b"".join(chunks_collected)
   429→
   430→                    usage = {}
   431→                    try:
   432→                        text = full_response.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
   433→                        for line in reversed(text.split("\n")):
   434→                            if "message_delta" in line and "usage" in line:
   435→                                if line.startswith("data: "):
   436→                                    event_data = json.loads(line[6:])
   437→                                    usage = event_data.get("usage", {})
   438→                                    break
   439→                            elif "message_start" in line and "usage" in line:
   440→                                if line.startswith("data: "):
   441→                                    event_data = json.loads(line[6:])
   442→                                    msg = event_data.get("message", {})
   443→                                    usage = msg.get("usage", {})
   444→                    except Exception:
   445→                        pass
   446→
   447→                    log_record({
   448→                        "type": "response_stream",
   449→                        "timestamp": response_time.isoformat(),
   450→                        "duration_ms": int(
   451→                            (response_time - request_time).total_seconds()
   452→                            * 1000
   453→                        ),
   454→                        "first_byte_ms": int(
   455→                            (first_byte_time - request_time).total_seconds()
   456→                            * 1000
   457→                        )
   458→                        if first_byte_time
   459→                        else None,
   460→                        "status_code": resp.status_code,
   461→                        "response_bytes": len(full_response),
   462→                        "usage": usage,
   463→                    })
   464→
   465→            return Response(
   466→                generate(),
   467→                status=resp.status_code,
   468→                headers=response_headers,
   469→                content_type=response_headers.get(
   470→                    "content-type", "text/event-stream"
   471→                ),
   472→            )
   473→        except Exception as e:
   474→            log_record({
   475→                "type": "proxy_error",
   476→                "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   477→                "error": str(e),
   478→            })
   479→            return Response(
   480→                json.dumps({"error": str(e)}),
   481→                status=502,
   482→                content_type="application/json",
   483→            )
   484→
   485→    @app.route("/health")
   486→    def health():
   487→        parts = []
   488→        if compact:
   489→            parts.append("compact")
   490→        if trim:
   491→            parts.append("trim")
   492→        mode = "+".join(parts) if parts else "observe"
   493→
   494→        result = {
   495→            "status": "ok",
   496→            "mode": mode,
   497→            "log_file": str(log_file),
   498→        }
   499→        if compact and page_store is not None:
   500→            result["pager"] = page_store.summary()
   501→        if trim and trimmer is not None:
   502→            result["trimmer"] = trimmer.summary()
   503→        return result
   504→
   505→    print(f"Logging to: {log_file}", file=sys.stderr)
   506→    return app
   507→
   508→
   509→def main():
   510→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   511→        description="Logging proxy for Claude API (with optional context paging)"
   512→    )
   513→    parser.add_argument(
   514→        "--port", type=int, default=0,
   515→        help="Port to listen on (0 = random free port, default: 0)",
   516→    )
   517→    parser.add_argument(
   518→        "--log-dir", type=Path, default=Path("logs"),
   519→        help="Directory for proxy logs (default: logs/)",
   520→    )
   521→    parser.add_argument(
   522→        "--compact", action="store_true",
   523→        help="Enable context paging: evict stale tool results",
   524→    )
   525→    parser.add_argument(
   526→        "--trim", action="store_true",
   527→        help="Enable system prompt trimming: tool stubs, skill dedup, static tracking",
   528→    )
   529→    parser.add_argument(
   530→        "--age-threshold", type=int, default=4,
   531→        help="Evict tool results older than N user-turns (default: 4)",
   532→    )
   533→    parser.add_argument(
   534→        "--min-size", type=int, default=500,
   535→        help="Don't evict results smaller than N bytes (default: 500)",
   536→    )
   537→    parser.add_argument(
   538→        "--temperature", type=float, default=None,
   539→        help="Override temperature on all requests (e.g., 0 for deterministic)",
   540→    )
   541→    args = parser.parse_args()
   542→
   543→    app = create_app(
   544→        args.log_dir,
   545→        compact=args.compact,
   546→        trim=args.trim,
   547→        age_threshold=args.age_threshold,
   548→        min_size=args.min_size,
   549→    )
   550→    if args.temperature is not None:
   551→        app.config["temperature_override"] = args.temperature
   552→
   553→    port = args.port if args.port != 0 else find_free_port()
   554→    parts = []
   555→    if args.compact:
   556→        parts.append("COMPACT")
   557→    if args.trim:
   558→        parts.append("TRIM")
   559→    mode = "+".join(parts) if parts else "OBSERVE"
   560→    print(
   561→        f"Proxy [{mode}] listening on http://localhost:{port}",
   562→        file=sys.stderr,
   563→    )
   564→    print(
   565→        f"Use: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:{port} claude",
   566→        file=sys.stderr,
   567→    )
   568→    app.run(host="127.0.0.1", port=port, threaded=True)
   569→
   570→
   571→if __name__ == "__main__":
   572→    main()
   573→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""Evaluation framework for context paging experiments.
     3→
     4→Parses proxy JSONL logs from experimental runs, computes per-turn and
     5→cumulative metrics, and compares across treatment conditions.
     6→
     7→Data sources:
     8→    - proxy_*.jsonl: request/response records with usage data
     9→    - pages_*.jsonl: eviction and page fault records
    10→
    11→Metrics computed:
    12→    - Token consumption (input, output, cache hits/misses)
    13→    - API call count
    14→    - Context size growth curve
    15→    - Compaction events and savings
    16→    - Fault rate
    17→    - Wall-clock time
    18→    - System prompt overhead
    19→
    20→Usage:
    21→    # Analyze a single run
    22→    uv run python tools/phase1/experiment_eval.py --run tmp/api_logs/proxy_*.jsonl
    23→
    24→    # Compare treatments
    25→    uv run python tools/phase1/experiment_eval.py \\
    26→        --compare baseline=tmp/exp/baseline/proxy.jsonl \\
    27→                  t1_compact=tmp/exp/t1/proxy.jsonl \\
    28→                  t2_trimmed=tmp/exp/t2/proxy.jsonl
    29→
    30→    # JSON output for downstream analysis
    31→    uv run python tools/phase1/experiment_eval.py --run proxy.jsonl --json
    32→"""
    33→
    34→from __future__ import annotations
    35→
    36→import argparse
    37→import json
    38→import sys
    39→from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
    40→from datetime import datetime
    41→from pathlib import Path
    42→
    43→
    44→@dataclass
    45→class TurnMetrics:
    46→    """Metrics for a single API call (request + response pair)."""
    47→
    48→    turn: int
    49→    timestamp: str
    50→    model: str
    51→    # Token counts from Anthropic's response
    52→    # input_tokens = non-cached only. Effective = input + cache_creation + cache_read
    53→    input_tokens: int = 0
    54→    output_tokens: int = 0
    55→    cache_creation_tokens: int = 0
    56→    cache_read_tokens: int = 0
    57→
    58→    @property
    59→    def effective_input_tokens(self) -> int:
    60→        """Total tokens in context = non-cached + cache_creation + cache_read."""
    61→        return self.input_tokens + self.cache_creation_tokens + self.cache_read_tokens
    62→    # Sizes from proxy measurement
    63→    total_request_bytes: int = 0
    64→    messages_bytes: int = 0
    65→    system_prompt_bytes: int = 0
    66→    tool_result_count: int = 0
    67→    tool_result_bytes: int = 0
    68→    tool_use_count: int = 0
    69→    # Compaction (if active)
    70→    evictions: int = 0
    71→    compaction_bytes_saved: int = 0
    72→    faults: int = 0
    73→    # Timing
    74→    duration_ms: int = 0
    75→    first_byte_ms: int = 0
    76→
    77→
    78→@dataclass
    79→class RunSummary:
    80→    """Aggregate metrics for one experimental run."""
    81→
    82→    label: str
    83→    proxy_log: str
    84→    # Totals
    85→    api_calls: int = 0
    86→    total_input_tokens: int = 0
    87→    total_output_tokens: int = 0
    88→    total_cache_creation: int = 0
    89→    total_cache_read: int = 0
    90→    total_tokens: int = 0  # input + output
    91→    total_effective_input: int = 0  # input + cache_creation + cache_read (context size)
    92→    # Bytes
    93→    total_request_bytes: int = 0
    94→    total_messages_bytes: int = 0
    95→    total_system_prompt_bytes: int = 0
    96→    total_tool_result_bytes: int = 0
    97→    # Compaction
    98→    total_evictions: int = 0
    99→    total_compaction_bytes_saved: int = 0
   100→    total_faults: int = 0
   101→    fault_rate: float = 0.0
   102→    # Timing
   103→    total_duration_ms: int = 0
   104→    wall_clock_seconds: float = 0.0
   105→    avg_first_byte_ms: float = 0.0
   106→    # Context growth
   107→    max_messages_bytes: int = 0
   108→    max_input_tokens: int = 0
   109→    # Per-turn data for curves
   110→    turns: list[TurnMetrics] = field(default_factory=list)
   111→
   112→    @property
   113→    def avg_input_tokens(self) -> float:
   114→        if self.api_calls == 0:
   115→            return 0.0
   116→        return self.total_input_tokens / self.api_calls
   117→
   118→    @property
   119→    def avg_effective_input(self) -> float:
   120→        if self.api_calls == 0:
   121→            return 0.0
   122→        return self.total_effective_input / self.api_calls
   123→
   124→    @property
   125→    def n_squared_cost(self) -> int:
   126→        """Cumulative effective input tokens — the n² metric."""
   127→        return self.total_effective_input
   128→
   129→    @property
   130→    def system_prompt_fraction(self) -> float:
   131→        if self.total_request_bytes == 0:
   132→            return 0.0
   133→        return self.total_system_prompt_bytes / self.total_request_bytes
   134→
   135→
   136→def parse_proxy_log(path: Path) -> list[dict]:
   137→    """Read all records from a proxy JSONL log."""
   138→    records = []
   139→    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as f:
   140→        for line in f:
   141→            line = line.strip()
   142→            if not line:
   143→                continue
   144→            try:
   145→                records.append(json.loads(line))
   146→            except json.JSONDecodeError:
   147→                continue
   148→    return records
   149→
   150→
   151→def parse_page_log(path: Path) -> list[dict]:
   152→    """Read eviction/fault records from a page log."""
   153→    if not path.exists():
   154→        return []
   155→    return parse_proxy_log(path)
   156→
   157→
